Tests strict mode behavior when declaring a variable named 'eval'.

# Purpose
This code declares a global variable named `eval` in JavaScript. The use of `"use strict"` at the beginning of the file enforces strict mode, which helps catch common coding errors and prevents the use of certain JavaScript features that are considered problematic. Declaring `eval` as a variable in strict mode is not allowed, as `eval` is a reserved keyword in JavaScript used to execute a string of JavaScript code. This code does not provide any functional implementation and serves as an example of a syntax error in strict mode.
